ideation: This sequence simulates the windup and throw of a baseball pitch.  The left arm will act as a counterbalance, while the right arm performs the main pitching motion.  The sequence will focus on expressing the power and controlled movement involved in a pitch.  I will leverage the existing examples to ensure smooth transitions and realistic arm movement given the robot's two-joint structure. Frame 1 shows a preparatory stance: the right arm is drawn back, slightly bent at the shoulder, and the left arm is extended outward for balance. Frame 2 emphasizes the power of the windup, with the right arm fully extended backward.  Frame 3 captures the release of the ball, with the right arm moving forward and downward in a fluid motion. Frame 4 shows a follow-through, with the right arm continuing its downward trajectory. Frame 5 returns both arms to a neutral resting position.

frames.beat: arms ready, right arm coils back for the pitch
the right arm swings forward powerfully
the ball is released with a downward snap
follow through with the right arm's motion
both arms return to a relaxed state
